candidateContexts.label: admin-basic-info price CRUD facade to ts-price-service
admin-basic-info CRUD facade to contacts, stations, trains, configs services
admin-basic-info security and CORS policy surface
admin-order facade routing orders by train-number prefix to ts-order-service or ts-order-other-service
admin-order security and CORS policy surface
admin-route facade creating/deleting routes with station-existence precondition against ts-station-service
admin-route security and CORS policy surface

candidateCovers.label: admin-basic-info admin CRUD surface measured against its security policy
admin-order routing surface measured against its security policy
admin-route management surface measured against its security policy
cross-service admin security/CORS convention across admin-basic-info, admin-order, admin-route

notes: Spring Boot bootstrap class with a load-balanced RestTemplate bean; no domain logic.
Five GET read paths under adminbasic are open without authentication while the rest of the service path requires ADMIN role.
Every mutating and read endpoint carries a method-level @CrossOrigin(origins="*") annotation in addition to the class-level CORS mapping in SecurityConfig.
basicPriceRate and firstClassPriceRate are double primitive fields, not String or BigDecimal.
Pure interface declaring the Response-returning CRUD contract implemented by AdminBasicInfoServiceImpl.
Every method is a pure pass-through RestTemplate call to a single downstream service keyed by getServiceUrl(name); no local status branching present here unlike the order service.
Registers under nacos application name ts-admin-basic-info-service on port 18767; swagger controllerPackage set to adminbasic.microservice (does not match the actual adminbasic.controller package).
Same Spring Boot bootstrap shape as the other admin services.
Unlike admin-basic-info, no GET path here is opened with permitAll; the whole adminorderservice path requires ADMIN role.
Only the getAllOrders endpoint carries @CrossOrigin here, unlike admin-basic-info-service where every endpoint carries it.
Interface contract implemented by AdminOrderServiceImpl.
getAllOrders always calls both ts-order-service and ts-order-other-service and merges results, while delete/update/add branch by trainNumber prefix G or D to pick exactly one of the two providers.
Registers under nacos application name ts-admin-order-service on port 16112.
Same Spring Boot bootstrap shape as the other admin services.
Same shape as admin-order: whole adminrouteservice path requires ADMIN role, no open GET path.
addRoute is documented as create-and-modify but exposed only as POST; there is no separate PUT endpoint on this controller.
Interface contract implemented by AdminRouteServiceImpl.
createAndModifyRoute only reaches ts-route-service after two upstream checks against ts-station-service both pass (membership in the stations list, then existence via checkStationsExists).
Registers under nacos application name ts-admin-route-service on port 16113.
Only the Spring Boot bootstrap class for admin-travel-service is in this chunk; its controller, service, and config files fall outside this worklist range.
